revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Iwayajiato Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Iwayajiato Kofun | native_name =岩屋寺跡古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Iwayajiato Kofun 1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Iwayajiato Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Iwayajiato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|22|N|133|00|20|E|region:JP-32|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|22|N|133|00|20|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Iwayajiato Kofun'''|岩屋寺跡古墳|}} is a cluster of two [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves, located in the {{ill|Tamayu, Shimane|en|Tamayu, Shimane|qid=Q3514716|lt=Tamayu|1=Tamayu, Shimane}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The cluster of tombs was collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1948.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172895|title=岩屋寺跡古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Iwayajiato Kofun is located at the foot of a hill called Iwaya. It was the site of a Buddhist temple called Iwaya-ji, which was removed in 1876. These horizontal tunnel tombs were excavated into the {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} bedrock of the hill and are located 12 meters apart in a north–south direction. The north tomb has a double [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], with an antechamber that is about 1.8 meters long, 2.4 meters wide and two meters high, with a three-step cut stone entrance. The south tomb has a single burial chamber, 2.3 meters in length, 2.9 meters in width, and about 2.3 meters in height. Both burial chambers are characterized by careful construction, shaped to resemble the stone chambers found in ''[[kofun]]'' Kofuns, with processing the ceiling into a house shape and embossing the boundary between the roof and the wall. Both have been open since antiquity, so no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been found. They are estimated to have been built in the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is located about 1.5 kilometers from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}} [[:en:Tamatsukurionsen Station|Tamatsukurionsen Station]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=literature&subtarget=literature&id=2576&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=青木 Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
